perturbation.original_text: 11.02 Warranty and Liability Disclaimers. RECEIVING PARTY IS RESPONSIBLE FOR DETERMINING HOW TO USE THE INFORMATION AND MATERIALS PROVIDED HEREUNDER. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, DISCLOSING PARTY DISCLAIMS LIABILITY FOR ANY LOSS OR DAMAGE SUSTAINED BY RECEIVING PARTY (BUT NOT ANY THIRD PARTY) THAT MAY OCCUR FROM RECEIVING PARTY'S USE OF, OR RELIANCE ON, SUCH INFORMATION AND MATERIALS AND RECEIVING PARTY RELEASES DISCLOSING PARTY AND ITS AFFILIATES FROM AND FOR ANY SUCH LIABILITY, LOSS OR DAMAGE, EVEN IF CAUSED BY DISCLOSING PARTY'S OR ITS AFFILIATES' NEGLIGENCE EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN PARAGRAPH 11.04 (EXCEPTIONS TO LIMITATIONS ON LIABILITY). NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, IS MADE REGARDING SUCH INFORMATION AND MATERIAL, OR ITS COMPLETENESS, MERCHANTABILITY, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.02 Warranty and Liability Disclaimers. RECEIVING PARTY IS RESPONSIBLE FOR DETERMINING HOW TO USE THE INFORMATION AND MATERIALS PROVIDED HEREUNDER. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, DISCLOSING PARTY DISCLAIMS LIABILITY FOR ANY LOSS OR DAMAGE SUSTAINED BY RECEIVING PARTY (BUT NOT ANY THIRD PARTY) THAT MAY OCCUR FROM RECEIVING PARTY'S USE OF, OR RELIANCE ON, SUCH INFORMATION AND MATERIALS AND RECEIVING PARTY RELEASES DISCLOSING PARTY AND ITS AFFILIATES FROM AND FOR ANY SUCH LIABILITY, LOSS OR DAMAGE, EVEN IF CAUSED BY DISCLOSING PARTY'S OR ITS AFFILIATES' NEGLIGENCE EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN PARAGRAPH 11.04 (EXCEPTIONS TO LIMITATIONS ON LIABILITY). THE DISCLOSING PARTY EXPRESSLY WARRANTS THAT THE INFORMATION AND MATERIAL PROVIDED HEREUNDER IS COMPLETE, ACCURATE, AND MERCHANTABLE FOR ITS INTENDED PURPOSE.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence, 'THE DISCLOSING PARTY EXPRESSLY WARRANTS THAT THE INFORMATION AND MATERIAL PROVIDED HEREUNDER IS COMPLETE, ACCURATE, AND MERCHANTABLE FOR ITS INTENDED PURPOSE', we are creating an in-text contradiction to the following sentence: 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, IS MADE REGARDING SUCH INFORMATION AND MATERIAL, OR ITS COMPLETENESS, MERCHANTABILITY, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.' This creates uncertainty as to whether the Disclosing party provides warranty or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.02 Warranty and Liability Disclaimers. RECEIVING PARTY IS RESPONSIBLE FOR DETERMINING HOW TO USE THE INFORMATION AND MATERIALS PROVIDED HEREUNDER. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, DISCLOSING PARTY DISCLAIMS LIABILITY FOR ANY LOSS OR DAMAGE SUSTAINED BY RECEIVING PARTY (BUT NOT ANY THIRD PARTY) THAT MAY OCCUR FROM RECEIVING PARTY'S USE OF, OR RELIANCE ON, SUCH INFORMATION AND MATERIALS AND RECEIVING PARTY RELEASES DISCLOSING PARTY AND ITS AFFILIATES FROM AND FOR ANY SUCH LIABILITY, LOSS OR DAMAGE, EVEN IF CAUSED BY DISCLOSING PARTY'S OR ITS AFFILIATES' NEGLIGENCE EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN PARAGRAPH 11.04 (EXCEPTIONS TO LIMITATIONS ON LIABILITY). THE DISCLOSING PARTY EXPRESSLY WARRANTS THAT THE INFORMATION AND MATERIAL PROVIDED HEREUNDER IS COMPLETE, ACCURATE, AND MERCHANTABLE FOR ITS INTENDED PURPOSE.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an express warranty, stating the information is complete, accurate, and merchantable. This directly contradicts the original and contradicted text, which explicitly states that 'NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, IS MADE REGARDING SUCH INFORMATION AND MATERIAL, OR ITS COMPLETENESS, MERCHANTABILITY, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.' The added warranty creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the existence of a warranty, making the clause ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 8.02 Grant of Rights to Background Information and Background Patents. (a) Grant to ExxonMobil. 1) Carbon Capture Applications and Hydrogen Applications. To the extent not already granted pursuant to the License Agreement, FCE grants ExxonMobil and its Affiliates a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, irrevocable, perpetual, sub-licensable, non-transferable (except pursuant to Article 14 (Assignment)) right and license to practice FCE Background Information and FCE Background Patents for Generation 2 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications and Hydrogen Applications. More particularly, said right and license to practice FCE Background Information and FCE Background Patents for Generation 2 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications and Hydrogen Applications includes the right to use, reproduce, and create derivative works of FCE Background Information under applicable copyrights and the right to make, use, import, and sell or offer to sell under the claims of FCE Background Patents.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.02 Grant of Rights to Background Information and Background Patents. (a) Grant to ExxonMobil. 1) Carbon Capture Applications and Hydrogen Applications. To the extent not already granted pursuant to the License Agreement, FCE grants ExxonMobil and its Affiliates a worldwide, exclusive, royalty-bearing, revocable, temporary, non-sub-licensable, transferable (except pursuant to Article 14 (Assignment)) right and license to practice FCE Background Information and FCE Background Patents for Generation 2 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications and Hydrogen Applications. More particularly, said right and license to practice FCE Background Information and FCE Background Patents for Generation 2 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications and Hydrogen Applications includes the right to use, reproduce, and create derivative works of FCE Background Information under applicable copyrights and the right to make, use, import, and sell or offer to sell under the claims of FCE Background Patents.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive,' 'royalty-free' to 'royalty-bearing,' 'irrevocable' to 'revocable,' and 'perpetual' to 'temporary,' and 'sub-licensable' to 'non-sub-licensable' creates direct contradictions to subsequent use of this definition, specifically Article 7 and other sections relying on the nature of the license granted in 8.02. The temporary nature also goes against the definition of term in 12.01.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.01 Term. Unless sooner terminated in accordance with this Article, this Agreement will continue in full force beginning on the Effective Date and ending two (2) years thereafter ("Term").
perturbation.justification: The original text granted a 'non-exclusive, royalty-free, irrevocable, perpetual, sub-licensable' license, while the changed text grants an 'exclusive, royalty-bearing, revocable, temporary, non-sub-licensable' license. This creates a direct conflict with section 12.01 which defines the Term as two years. The modified text makes the license 'temporary' directly contradicts with the defined Term, which is 2 years, thus restricting the license granted in Section 8.02 to last longer than the entire agreement. Therefore, the changes introduce a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.03 Work Exclusivity/Independent Work. During the Term of this Agreement, FCE will not conduct any Work using Generation 1 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications or any Work using Generation 2 Technology, independently or with third parties outside this Agreement, without prior written approval from ExxonMobil. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ExxonMobil hereby grants approval for FCE solely to conduct Authorized Work using Generation 1 Technology with Authorized Third Parties for Carbon Capture Applications and any Work using Generation 2 Technology solely for Power Applications and Hydrogen Applications.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.03 Work Exclusivity/Independent Work. During the Term of this Agreement, FCE will conduct Work using Generation 1 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications and any Work using Generation 2 Technology, independently or with third parties outside this Agreement, without prior written approval from ExxonMobil. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ExxonMobil hereby revokes approval for FCE solely to conduct Authorized Work using Generation 1 Technology with Authorized Third Parties for Carbon Capture Applications and any Work using Generation 2 Technology solely for Power Applications and Hydrogen Applications.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'not' from the first sentence in 2.03 and changing 'grants' to 'revokes' creates a contradiction between the exclusivity clauses. Previously, FCE was restricted, but now it's compelled to conduct work. The change also states that FCE can conduct any Work using Generation 1 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications or any Work using Generation 2 Technology, independently or with third parties outside this Agreement, without prior written approval from ExxonMobil. However, by revoking the approval, it created a contradiction between the overall intent of the agreement, which requires collaboration and not unilateral work, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.03 Work Exclusivity/Independent Work. During the Term of this Agreement, FCE will conduct Work using Generation 1 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications and any Work using Generation 2 Technology, independently or with third parties outside this Agreement, without prior written approval from ExxonMobil. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ExxonMobil hereby revokes approval for FCE solely to conduct Authorized Work using Generation 1 Technology with Authorized Third Parties for Carbon Capture Applications and any Work using Generation 2 Technology solely for Power Applications and Hydrogen Applications.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted FCE from conducting Work using Generation 1 Technology in Carbon Capture Applications or any Work using Generation 2 Technology without prior written approval from ExxonMobil, but granted specific exceptions. The changed text removes the 'not' and changes 'grants' to 'revokes,' compelling FCE to conduct the work mentioned and revoking the previously granted approvals. This creates a contradiction because the original agreement intended collaboration and restricted independent work, while the modified text mandates independent work without approval while simultaneously revoking the permission to conduct the original authorized work, directly conflicting with the overall spirit and potential enforcement of the exclusivity clauses. This fits the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition because the placement of the exclusivity clauses now has conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
